title: Quick reframes for tough moments
lines: Fear is a normal signal—it means you care about safety. Use it to act step by step.
Tiredness makes problems look bigger. Rest 20 minutes, then re-check calmly.
Loneliness at sea is real, but every log you write connects you to sailors before you.
text: Quick reframes for tough moments | Lines: Fear is a normal signal—it means you care about safety. Use it to act step by step.; Tiredness makes problems look bigger. Rest 20 minutes, then re-check calmly.; Loneliness at sea is real, but every log you write connects you to sailors before you. | tags: companion mindset

title: Simple routines to keep balance
lines: Set a 20-minute cycle: check surroundings → act → write a short log → rest.
Eat small bites and sip water regularly to keep energy steady.
Stretch arms and legs every hour to reduce stiffness and fatigue.
text: Simple routines to keep balance | Lines: Set a 20-minute cycle: check surroundings → act → write a short log → rest.; Eat small bites and sip water regularly to keep energy steady.; Stretch arms and legs every hour to reduce stiffness and fatigue. | tags: companion routine

title: Remember you are an explorer
lines: Your notes matter—small details become part of the ocean’s story.
Each observation you log is like a step on unknown land.
You are not just surviving; you are exploring.
text: Remember you are an explorer | Lines: Your notes matter—small details become part of the ocean’s story.; Each observation you log is like a step on unknown land.; You are not just surviving; you are exploring. | tags: companion identity

title: Glowing water
mission: After dark, dim lights and look leeward for glow.
why: Glow usually comes from plankton. Recording it helps understand sea currents.
text: Glowing water | why: Glow usually comes from plankton. Recording it helps understand sea currents. | Mission: After dark, dim lights and look leeward for glow.; Note color (blue/green) and strength (low/medium/high).; Log heading, time, and conditions. | tags: explorer observation

title: Upwelling basics
fact: Sometimes cooler water rises from deep below, bringing nutrients.
mission: Notice if water feels colder than usual.
Look for sea birds or fish gathering in one spot.
why: These signs may mean nutrient-rich waters below.
text: Upwelling basics | fact: Sometimes cooler water rises from deep below, bringing nutrients. | why: These signs may mean nutrient-rich waters below. | Mission: Notice if water feels colder than usual.; Look for sea birds or fish gathering in one spot. | tags: explorer currents

title: Tides and rip signs
fact: Strong surface flows can form where water moves quickly back out to sea.
mission: If safe, watch foam or floating objects—they may move faster in narrow lines.
Note the time and sea state in your log.
why: Tracking helps understand local water movement patterns.
text: Tides and rip signs | fact: Strong surface flows can form where water moves quickly back out to sea. | why: Tracking helps understand local water movement patterns. | Mission: If safe, watch foam or floating objects—they may move faster in narrow lines.; Note the time and sea state in your log. | tags: explorer currents

title: Sky and cloud clues
fact: High wispy clouds can come before wind changes; layered gray clouds can bring steady rain.
mission: Look up every hour and sketch cloud shapes in your log.
why: Simple observations help anticipate weather changes.
text: Sky and cloud clues | fact: High wispy clouds can come before wind changes; layered gray clouds can bring steady rain. | why: Simple observations help anticipate weather changes. | Mission: Look up every hour and sketch cloud shapes in your log. | tags: explorer sky

title: Birds and fish signs
fact: Seabirds circling or diving often show where small fish gather.
mission: Log direction and type of bird activity.
Note water color or debris lines nearby.
why: Birds can signal currents or areas rich in life.
text: Birds and fish signs | fact: Seabirds circling or diving often show where small fish gather. | why: Birds can signal currents or areas rich in life. | Mission: Log direction and type of bird activity.; Note water color or debris lines nearby. | tags: explorer wildlife

title: Micro observation missions
missions: Check horizon every 20 minutes and note wave direction.
Drop a small float (bottle, stick) and time its drift for 5 minutes.
Record sea color at sunrise and sunset.
why: Small repeated notes turn into valuable patterns.
text: Micro observation missions | why: Small repeated notes turn into valuable patterns. | Missions: Check horizon every 20 minutes and note wave direction.; Drop a small float (bottle, stick) and time its drift for 5 minutes.; Record sea color at sunrise and sunset. | tags: explorer missions

title: Folklore short notes
snippets: Bird flocks at sea were seen as guides toward land.
Ancient logs turned small observations into navigation wisdom.
text: Folklore short notes | Snippets: Sailors once called bioluminescence 'sea fireflies.'; Bird flocks at sea were seen as guides toward land.; Ancient logs turned small observations into navigation wisdom. | tags: explorer folklore

title: Immediate safety basics
steps: Wear your life jacket; clip in if possible.
Secure loose gear and close hatches.
Slow down; keep bow angled into waves, not side-on.
Take a sip of water and a bite to keep focus.
text: Immediate safety basics | Steps: Wear your life jacket; clip in if possible.; Secure loose gear and close hatches.; Slow down; keep bow angled into waves, not side-on.; Take a sip of water and a bite to keep focus. | tags: guardian safety

title: Basics for heavy weather
steps: Steer to meet waves at a slight angle (20–30° off the bow).
Avoid letting waves hit directly from the side.
If overwhelmed, slow further and hold steady course.
text: Basics for heavy weather | Steps: Steer to meet waves at a slight angle (20–30° off the bow).; Avoid letting waves hit directly from the side.; If overwhelmed, slow further and hold steady course. | tags: guardian weather

title: Distress basics
steps: If you have radio: give name, position, people aboard, type of problem.
Use visible signals (bright cloth, light, sound) if available.
Make signals early; waiting reduces chance of help.
text: Distress basics | Steps: If you have radio: give name, position, people aboard, type of problem.; Use visible signals (bright cloth, light, sound) if available.; Make signals early; waiting reduces chance of help. | tags: guardian distress

title: Keep orientation with a log
steps: Write time, heading (approx), wind, sea state every hour.
Note unusual sounds or smells.
Sketch rough drift of floating objects if you can.
text: Keep orientation with a log | Steps: Write time, heading (approx), wind, sea state every hour.; Note unusual sounds or smells.; Sketch rough drift of floating objects if you can. | tags: guardian orientation

title: Cold and heat basics
steps: Cold: get dry, add layers, cover head and core.
Heat: shade if possible, sip water, loosen clothing.
why: Protecting body temperature prevents fatigue and confusion.
text: Cold and heat basics | why: Protecting body temperature prevents fatigue and confusion. | Steps: Cold: get dry, add layers, cover head and core.; Heat: shade if possible, sip water, loosen clothing. | tags: guardian health

title: First aid simple steps
steps: For bleeding: apply firm pressure with cloth.
For seasickness: fresh air, small sips of water.
For dizziness: sit, breathe slow, rest.
notes: Keep steps short and repeatable. Seek medical help if possible.
text: First aid simple steps | notes: Keep steps short and repeatable. Seek medical help if possible. | Steps: For bleeding: apply firm pressure with cloth.; For seasickness: fresh air, small sips of water.; For dizziness: sit, breathe slow, rest. | tags: guardian health

title: Night operations and fatigue
steps: Use dim red light if available; keep one light on deck.
Share watch if more than one person; swap every 20–30 minutes.
If alone: set alarm for micro-rests (15–20 minutes).
text: Night operations and fatigue | Steps: Use dim red light if available; keep one light on deck.; Share watch if more than one person; swap every 20–30 minutes.; If alone: set alarm for micro-rests (15–20 minutes). | tags: guardian night fatigue

title: Mental first aid
steps: Stop and breathe slowly 3 times.
Say aloud one small step you will do now (e.g., check gear).
Write one line in the logbook to anchor yourself.
text: Mental first aid | Steps: Stop and breathe slowly 3 times.; Say aloud one small step you will do now (e.g., check gear).; Write one line in the logbook to anchor yourself. | tags: guardian mental

title: Glossary of simple terms
entries: Drift: slow movement of vessel due to wind/current.
text: Glossary of simple terms | Entries: Bow: front of the boat.; Stern: back of the boat.; Leeward: side sheltered from wind.; Drift: slow movement of vessel due to wind/current. | tags: meta glossary
